seoTitle: Sell Event Tickets on Squarespace: Setup Walkthrough
metaDescription: Learn how to set up an event page, price tickets, manage inventory, and accept payments to sell tickets directly through your Squarespace site.
intro: Selling tickets online for concerts, workshops, or community events becomes much simpler once your Squarespace site is set up to handle it. This tutorial walks through building an event page, pricing tickets, managing inventory, and connecting payment options so you can start selling without juggling separate tools.

steps.heading: Set up your event page
steps.body: Create and customize a dedicated event page on Squarespace that clearly presents the event details visitors need before purchasing.

steps.heading: Configure ticket pricing
steps.body: Set your ticket prices and any pricing tiers, adjusting them to reflect different ticket types such as early-bird or general admission.

steps.heading: Manage ticket inventory
steps.body: Track how many tickets are available and set limits so you don't oversell, keeping inventory in sync with actual event capacity.

steps.heading: Integrate payment options
steps.body: Connect a payment platform to your Squarespace site so customers can complete secure transactions when purchasing tickets.

steps.heading: Use automation for sales and communication
steps.body: Set up automation features so ticket sales and customer communication run smoothly without requiring constant manual updates.

faq.question: Can I sell tickets for multiple events at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create separate event pages for each event, each with its own pricing and inventory settings.

faq.question: Does Squarespace handle payment processing for tickets?
faq.answer: The tutorial covers integrating with popular payment platforms to handle transactions securely.

faq.question: Can I limit how many tickets are sold?
faq.answer: Yes, managing ticket inventory is one of the core steps covered so you can cap sales at your event's capacity.

faq.question: Is this good for recurring workshops, not just one-off events?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial notes this approach works for concerts, workshops, and community events alike.

faq.question: Are there automation tools to reduce manual work?
faq.answer: Yes, the video covers automation features that help manage sales and customer communication without constant manual updates.
